Figures included in visual abstract

Lucien Bickerstaff

2026-06-08

# Seed for reproducibility
seed <- 2025
set.seed(seed)

# Libraries
library(here)
library(openxlsx)
library(photobiology)
library(tidyverse)
library(grid)
library(lme4)
library(patchwork)

source(here("R", "helpers.R"))
source(here("R", "plot_settings.R"))

Normalised SPDs of primaries used

# Import spectral data
spec_data <- read.xlsx(here("data", "light_source_calibration", "1_raw", "2b_target_settings_corrected_raw.xlsx"))
# Transpose the data
spec_data <- as.data.frame(t(spec_data), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
# Assign the first row as header
colnames(spec_data) <- spec_data[1, ]
spec_data <- spec_data[-1, , drop = FALSE]

Extract spectral data

spec_data <- spec_data %>%
  select(c(Name, "380":"780"))

spec_data <- spec_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -1,                        # all columns except the first
    names_to = "wavelength",          # column for wavelength names
    values_to = "Ee [W/(sqm*nm)]"           # column for measured values
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(  # ensure numeric x-axis
    -1,  # skip the first column (the names)
    ~ as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", .))
  )) %>% 
  mutate(
    measurement = str_extract(Name, "(?<=#)\\d+"),
    measurement = factor(measurement),
    measurement = fct_reorder(measurement, as.numeric(as.character(measurement))),
    measurement_num = as.numeric(measurement)
  ) %>%
  select(-Name)
gradient <- t(w_length2rgb(400:700))
g <- rasterGrob(gradient, width = unit(1, "npc"), height = unit(1, "npc"), interpolate = TRUE)

# Manual peak wavelengths (from the product manual)
primary_wavelengths <- c(
  "2" = 445,
  "3" = 465,
  "4" = 475,
  "5" = 505,
  "6" = 525,
  "7" = 545
)

# Label positions using manual peak wavelengths
label_data <- data.frame(
  primary    = names(primary_wavelengths),
  wavelength = as.numeric(primary_wavelengths),
  label_y    = 1.08
)

# Gradient for line colours
wavelength_seq <- 380:780
spectrum_cols <- sapply(wavelength_seq, w_length2rgb)

gradient_scale <- scale_colour_gradientn(
  colours = spectrum_cols,
  values  = scales::rescale(wavelength_seq),
  name    = "Wavelength (nm)"
)

plot_data <- spec_data %>% 
  mutate(
    primary = case_when(
      measurement_num == 28 ~ "2",
      measurement_num == 32 ~ "3",
      measurement_num == 36 ~ "4",
      measurement_num == 40 ~ "5",
      measurement_num == 44 ~ "6",
      measurement_num == 48 ~ "7"
    )
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::filter(measurement_num %in% c(28, 32, 36, 40, 44, 48)) %>% 
  group_by(primary) %>% 
  mutate(Ee_norm = `Ee [W/(sqm*nm)]` / max(`Ee [W/(sqm*nm)]`))

plot_spectra <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = wavelength, y = Ee_norm, colour = wavelength, group = primary)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.7, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "darkred") +
  
  # Vertical dashed lines at manual peak wavelengths
  geom_segment(
  data = data.frame(wavelength = primary_wavelengths),
  aes(x = wavelength, xend = wavelength, y = 0, yend = 1),
  linetype = "dashed",
  colour = "black",
  alpha = 0.6,
  inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  # Manual labels at manual peak wavelengths
  geom_text(
    data = label_data,
    aes(x = wavelength, y = label_y, label = primary),
    colour = "black",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 4,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  gradient_scale +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
    limits = c(0, 1.08),
    labels = function(x) ifelse(x %% 1 == 0, formatC(x, format = "f", digits = 0), x)
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Wavelength (nm)",
    y = "Relative irradiance",
    title = "Spectral distribution"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 25, l = 10), # need to avoid clipping on right-hand side
    axis.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 5))
    ) +
  guides(colour = "none") +
  plot_formatting
plot_spectra

Hypothesis plots

Need to simulate data. We run the simulation once again (same as for sensitivity analysis)

m_mel <- readRDS(here("outputs", "models", "emg_lmm_mel.rds"))

model_frame <- m_mel$frame

observed_log_baseline <- model_frame$log_baseline

design_matrix <- read_csv(here("outputs", "design_matrix.csv"))
## Rows: 14400 Columns: 17
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr  (1): color
## dbl (16): sequence_id, trial, debruijn_label, assigned_condition, participan...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
design_matrix <- readr::read_csv(
  here::here("outputs", "design_matrix.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    participant_id = factor(participant_id),
    lab_visit = factor(lab_visit),

    melanopic_edi = MEDI,
    rhodopic_edi = REDI,

    # Must match the transformation used when fitting the EMG model.
    # Even though there is no 0-lx condition here
    log_illuminance = log10(pmax(illuminance, 1)),
    log_melanopic_edi = log10(pmax(melanopic_edi, 1)),
    log_rhodopic_edi  = log10(pmax(rhodopic_edi, 1)),

    lights_on = melanopic_edi != 0
  ) %>% 
  # Edit it to have 20 participants only
  dplyr::filter(as.integer(as.character(participant_id)) < 21)

simulate_from_design <- function(design_df,
                                 model,
                                 sd_scaling = 1,
                                 seed = NULL,
                                 baseline_pool = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(seed)) {
    set.seed(seed)
  }

  if (!requireNamespace("MASS", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'MASS' is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'lme4' is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!requireNamespace("glmmTMB", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'glmmTMB' is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Prepare and check design matrix
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  dat <- design_df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      participant_id = factor(participant_id),
      trial = as.numeric(trial),
      lights_on = as.logical(lights_on)
    )
  
  # Optionally resample log_baseline for every row/trial.
  # Because this happens inside simulate_from_design(), each simulation replicate
  # receives a fresh set of baseline values.
  if (!is.null(baseline_pool)) {
    
    baseline_pool <- baseline_pool[is.finite(baseline_pool)]
    
    if (length(baseline_pool) == 0) {
      stop(
        "`baseline_pool` contains no finite values.",
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }
    
    dat <- dat %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        log_baseline = sample(
          baseline_pool,
          size = dplyr::n(),
          replace = TRUE
        )
      )
  }

  # Variables needed by the model RHS, including random-effect grouping variables
  required_vars <- all.vars(stats::formula(model)[[3]])

  missing_vars <- setdiff(required_vars, names(dat))

  if (length(missing_vars) > 0) {
    stop(
      "The design matrix is missing variables required by the model: ",
      paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", "),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  # Check for missing predictor values
  predictor_na_counts <- dat %>%
    dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(required_vars)) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      dplyr::across(
        dplyr::everything(),
        ~ sum(is.na(.x))
      )
    )

  if (any(predictor_na_counts > 0)) {
    print(predictor_na_counts)
    stop(
      "Some model predictors contain NA values in the design matrix.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Fixed-effect prediction
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  # Fixed-effects-only prediction on the model scale.
  # For a Gaussian identity-link model fitted to log_emg_raw, this is the
  # predicted log_emg_raw value without participant-specific random effects.
  dat$eta_fixed <- predict(
    model,
    newdata = dat,
    re.form = NA,
    type = "link",
    allow.new.levels = TRUE
  )

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Simulate random effects
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  dat$eta_random <- 0

  random_terms <- lme4::findbars(stats::formula(model))

  vc <- glmmTMB::VarCorr(model)$cond

  for (term in random_terms) {

    random_effect_formula <- term[[2]]
    grouping_factor_expr  <- term[[3]]

    grouping_vars <- all.vars(grouping_factor_expr)
    grouping_name <- paste(grouping_vars, collapse = ":")

    if (!grouping_name %in% names(vc)) {
      warning(
        "Random-effect grouping factor '", grouping_name,
        "' not found in VarCorr(model). Skipping this random effect."
      )
      next
    }

    vc_matrix <- as.matrix(vc[[grouping_name]])

    # Examples:
    #   (1 | participant_id) becomes ~ 1
    #   (log_melanopic_edi | participant_id) becomes ~ log_melanopic_edi
    # Because model.matrix(~ x) includes the intercept, this correctly gives
    # random intercept + random slope for (x | participant_id).
    re_formula <- stats::as.formula(
      paste("~", paste(deparse(random_effect_formula), collapse = ""))
    )

    Z <- stats::model.matrix(re_formula, data = dat)

    group_id <- if (length(grouping_vars) == 1) {
      dat[[grouping_vars]]
    } else {
      interaction(dat[grouping_vars], drop = TRUE)
    }

    group_id <- factor(group_id)

    simulated_re <- MASS::mvrnorm(
      n = nlevels(group_id),
      mu = rep(0, ncol(vc_matrix)),
      Sigma = vc_matrix
    )

    simulated_re <- as.matrix(simulated_re)

    colnames(simulated_re) <- colnames(vc_matrix)
    rownames(simulated_re) <- levels(group_id)

    common_cols <- intersect(colnames(Z), colnames(simulated_re))

    if (length(common_cols) == 0) {
      warning(
        "No matching random-effect columns for grouping factor '",
        grouping_name,
        "'. Skipping this random effect."
      )
      next
    }

    re_contribution <- rowSums(
      Z[, common_cols, drop = FALSE] *
        simulated_re[as.character(group_id), common_cols, drop = FALSE]
    )

    dat$eta_random <- dat$eta_random + re_contribution
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Simulate residual noise and back-transform response
  # Floor is applied to the expected response before residual noise is added
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  sd_resid <- stats::sigma(model) * sd_scaling

  # Fixed-effect floor at 0 lx, computed from the original data used to fit
  # the model, not from the new simulation design.
  model_frame <- stats::model.frame(model)

  if (!"lights_on" %in% names(model_frame)) {
    stop(
      "Could not compute baseline floor because `lights_on` is not in the model frame.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  model_frame <- model_frame %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      lights_on = as.logical(lights_on)
    )

  baseline_model_frame <- model_frame %>%
    dplyr::filter(!lights_on)

  if (nrow(baseline_model_frame) == 0) {
    stop(
      "Could not compute baseline floor because the model frame contains no lights_on == FALSE rows.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  baseline_floor_log10 <- mean(
    predict(
      model,
      newdata = baseline_model_frame,
      re.form = NA,
      type = "link",
      allow.new.levels = TRUE
    ),
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  if (!is.finite(baseline_floor_log10)) {
    stop(
      "Could not compute a finite baseline floor from the model frame.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  baseline_floor_raw <- 10^baseline_floor_log10

  dat <- dat %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      # Expected response from fixed + random effects, before flooring
      expected_log10_unfloored = eta_fixed + eta_random,
  
      # Indicator: was this row affected by the floor?
      floored = expected_log10_unfloored < baseline_floor_log10,
  
      # Expected response after applying physiological floor
      expected_log10 = pmax(
        expected_log10_unfloored,
        baseline_floor_log10
      ),
  
      # Use the same residual-noise draw for both versions,
      # so flooring is the only difference
      residual_noise = stats::rnorm(
        n = dplyr::n(),
        mean = 0,
        sd = sd_resid
      ),
  
      simulated_log10_unfloored = expected_log10_unfloored + residual_noise,
      simulated_log10 = expected_log10 + residual_noise,
  
      simulated_emg_unfloored = 10^simulated_log10_unfloored,
      simulated_emg = 10^simulated_log10,
  
      baseline_floor_log10 = baseline_floor_log10,
      baseline_floor_emg = baseline_floor_raw
    )

  dat
}

sim_dat <- simulate_from_design(
  design_df = design_matrix, 
  model = m_mel, 
  sd_scaling = 1, 
  baseline_pool = observed_log_baseline,
  seed = seed)
## Warning in check_dep_version(dep_pkg = "TMB"): package version mismatch: 
## glmmTMB was built with TMB package version 1.9.19
## Current TMB package version is 1.9.21
## Please re-install glmmTMB from source or restore original 'TMB' package (see '?reinstalling' for more information)
## Warning: the 'findbars' function has moved to the reformulas package. Please update your imports, or ask an upstream package maintainter to do so.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
sim_dat_plot <- sim_dat %>% 
  mutate(primary = as.factor(as.numeric(primary)), # Reset factor labels to 1-6 instead of 2-7
         wavelength = primary_wavelengths[primary]) %>% # add wavelength per primary 
  group_by(primary, lab_visit) %>% 
  summarise(mean_response = mean(simulated_log10, na.rm = TRUE),
            # mean_response_norm  = mean_response / max(response_sim, na.rm = TRUE),
            illuminance = first(illuminance), 
            MEDI = first(MEDI),
            wavelength = first(wavelength),
            .groups = "drop")

label_dat <- sim_dat_plot %>%
  group_by(primary, wavelength) %>%
  do({
    fit <- lm(mean_response ~ illuminance, data = .)
    tibble(
      x = max(.$illuminance),
      y = predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(
        illuminance = max(.$illuminance)
      ))
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(label = as.character(primary))   # 1–6 labels

plot_ee <- ggplot(sim_dat_plot, aes(x = illuminance, y = mean_response, colour = wavelength, group = primary)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  # Labels next to fitted lines
  annotate("text", x = 200, y = -2.2, label = "2", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 500, y = -2.15, label = "3", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 1000, y = -2.125, label = "4", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 3000, y = -2.13, label = "5", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 5000, y = -2.2, label = "6", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 4000, y = -2.28, label = "7", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = breaks, minor_breaks = minor_breaks) +
  scale_color_continuous(palette = c("navyblue", "chartreuse3")) +
  labs(x = "Illuminance (lux)",
       y = "Predicted EMG response",
       colour = "Wavelength",
       group = "Primary") +
  theme_minimal() +
  plot_formatting
plot_ee
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

plot_medi <- ggplot(sim_dat_plot, aes(x = MEDI, y = mean_response, colour = wavelength, group = primary)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_color_continuous(palette = c("navyblue", "chartreuse3")) +
  labs(x = "Melanopic EDI (lux)",
       y = "Predicted EMG response",
       colour = "Wavelength\n(nm)",
       group = "Primary") +
  theme_minimal() +
  plot_formatting

plot_hypotheses <- 
  plot_ee + guides(colour = "none") + labs(title = "Expected results") + plot_formatting +
  plot_medi + (labs(y = NULL)) + plot_formatting
plot_hypotheses
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

plot_light_metrics <- readRDS(here("outputs", "figures", "rds", "calibration_melanopic_vs_photopic.rds")) +
  labs(y = "Illuminance (lux)")
plot_light_metrics

Saving all plots

fixed_height <- 3 # so all plots can be scaled identically

formatted_save(
  here("outputs", "figures", "abstract_spectra_plot.jpg"), 
  plot = plot_spectra, 
  width = 6,
  height = fixed_height
)
save_rds_safe(plot_spectra, here("outputs", "figures", "rds", "abstract_spectra_plot.rds"))

formatted_save(
  here("outputs", "figures", "abstract_hypotheses_plot.jpg"), 
  plot = plot_hypotheses,
  width = 5.5,
  height = fixed_height
)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
save_rds_safe(plot_spectra, here("outputs", "figures", "rds", "abstract_hypotheses_plot.rds"))

formatted_save(
  here("outputs", "figures", "abstract_light_metrics.jpg"), 
  plot = plot_light_metrics, 
  width = 4,
  height = fixed_height
)
save_rds_safe(plot_light_metrics, here("outputs", "figures", "rds", "abstract_light_metrics.rds"))
sessionInfo()
